Pillar 4: Conversion Contexts
At its core, the success of your coaching or course business depends on getting a ōyesö on THREE invitations. 

Invitation 1: To invest time and energy in you and your message (aka the opt-in or subscribe)

Invitation 2: To invest time, energy and money in your program (aka the purchase)

Invitation 3: To invest time, energy and money in implementing it (aka the ōstickö - LTV) 

Chances are, youÆre making all three invitations. Maybe not as strategically as youÆd like. Maybe not as frequently as youÆd like. 

But if youÆre reading this, you are making them. 

What gets missed is that getting a ōyesö has less to do with making an invitation itself, as it does the CONTEXT in which the invitation is made. 

ie. A marriage proposal after a night at the in-laws where you slept in separate beds and felt triggered every twenty seconds is far less likely to result in a ōyesö than a marriage proposal made on the first night of a coupleÆs retreat with an oceanview in your luxury suite. 

Same couple. Same core desires, dreams and ōdestinyö. Wrong context. 
When youÆre making life-changing invitations to your prospect; the context matters as much, if not more than the invitation itself. 

Yet up until very recently, marketing technology didnÆt give us the intelligence or the ability to make contextually-relevant invitations. 

Want to ōengineerö the perfect moment for a life-changing offer? 

Your prospect should be feeling: 

1. Positive surprise / magic / synchronicity (a prevailing sense that this 

2. Witnessed / Appreciated / Seen / Heard

3. Self-Confidence

4. Clarity over what they want 

5. Connection to their power and ability to achieve it

6. Connection with you as someone who can lead them (aka resonance)

7. Security (that itÆs a safe decision and theyÆll be seen favorably for making it).

Your ōfunnelö only creates a basic container where these elements are made possible.

But a funnel, on its own, lacks the beating heart and dynamic flow to fully create the context that provides all 7 elements. 

ThatÆs why a paltry 2% conversion rate is considered ōgoodö. But hereÆs the important paradigm shift you need to accept if youÆre going to take your conversion rates into the double digits. 

A funnel's primary job isnÆt to create a final conversion... 

ItÆs to create feedback loops and collect data that signal to you when itÆs time to intervene in a more personalized touchpoint. 

Example 1: A funnel gives you the chance to turn a total stranger into an email subscriber. This in of itself holds a ōlittleÆ bit of value. But when your CRM feeds you this data, you have a chance to send a 90 second personalized video welcoming the new member and opening up a more intimate conversation that can drastically increase the odds of a sale downstream. 

Example 2: An automated email sequence will be somewhat successful in coaching a new subscriber towards a sale. But when that same sequence helps you identify WHO is opening and clicking in all the emails; and even consuming your lead magnet; you have a chance to personally intervene and mirror back their high commitment while powerfully coaching and guiding their next steps. 

Your funnel is therefore a starting point. ItÆs the scaffolding that creates a degree of structural integrity to an otherwise un-contained ecosystem. 
But itÆs what happens within it; and all the dynamic actions, behaviors and intentions our prospect shows; that really sets us off on the path towards record-breaking conversion numbers. 

This process is empowered through what we call Automated Intimacy.
